DataProcessor.description:
A data processor entry in a data protection policy.

Represents a sub-processor under Art. 28 GDPR. Name and service identify the
vendor; `dpa_status` tracks the Auftragsverarbeitungsvertrag (DPA) lifecycle.

data_categories.description: Categories of personal data processed (e.g. "user account", "telemetry").

dpa_status.description: DPA execution status per Art. 28 GDPR.

hosting_region.description: Primary hosting region or country for the processed data (e.g. "EU (Frankfurt)", "US").

service.description: Human-readable description of the service provided (e.g. "auth, database, storage").

DpaStatus.description: Data processing agreement (DPA) execution status for a processor under Art. 28 GDPR.

oneOf.description: DPA is executed and in force.
DPA is required and drafted but not yet executed.
DPA is under negotiation with the counterparty.
Controller has determined no DPA is needed (e.g. no personal data exchanged).
DPA was executed but has lapsed.
Catch-all for statuses not covered above; prefer a specific variant when possible.

PathRef.description:
Path-based cross-reference relative to .corpospec/ root.
Pattern: `^[a-z0-9_-]+(/[a-z0-9_.-]+)+$`

processors.description: Sub-processors covered by this policy, tracked for Art. 28 GDPR compliance.

description.description: One-paragraph summary of the policy's purpose and the obligations it imposes.

owner.description:
PathRef into `people/**` for the person accountable for this policy — reviewing,
updating, and enforcing it.

scope.description:
Population and data the policy applies to. Prose description of the in-scope
population (e.g. "All full-time employees"), systems, or data categories.
Common categories include company-wide, engineering, customer-data,
employee-data, and third-party-processors.
